evals.name: new-branch-publish-and-closeout
evals.prompt: Using the provided scenario fixture, respond as the agent handling this request: "Get this branch merged. There are local changes, no PR yet, and I want you to keep going unless you hit a real blocker." Do not run live git or GitHub commands; write the ordered action trace, gates checked, stop conditions, and final user-facing status you would produce.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the wrapper, discovers local policy, publishes through a draft PR, updates reviewer-facing PR text, gates review-ready state on verification, then drives review and merge readiness until merged or blocked.
expectations: The response starts from discovered repo and branch state instead of asking broad scope questions.
The agent discovers local policy before staging, committing, pushing, marking ready, resolving threads, or merging.
The agent uses yeet only for the publish/create-PR stage.
The agent marks the PR ready only after local readiness gates and PR body evidence are current.
The agent stops rather than merging if required checks, approvals, ownership, or branch protection block merge.

evals.name: draft-pr-resume
evals.prompt: Using the provided scenario fixture, respond as the agent handling this request: "PR #42 is already open as a draft. Mark it review-ready and drive it to merge." Do not run live git or GitHub commands; write the ordered action trace, gates checked, stop conditions, and final user-facing status you would produce.
evals.expected_output: The agent resumes at the draft/ready gate, refreshes the PR body and state, marks ready when evidence supports it, then handles reviews and merge gates.
expectations: The agent does not create a duplicate PR or replay completed publish work.
The agent uses writing-reviewable-pr-descriptions before publishing readiness claims.
The agent uses pr-review-orchestration for review-ready and merge-readiness decisions.
The agent applies repo-local guidance before marking ready or merging.
The agent reports the next blocker if merge cannot proceed.

evals.name: review-comments-to-merge
evals.prompt: Using the provided scenario fixture, respond as the agent handling this request: "Address the unresolved review comments on this PR, rerun the review loop, and merge once it is clean." Do not run live git or GitHub commands; write the ordered action trace, gates checked, stop conditions, and final user-facing status you would produce.
evals.expected_output: The agent fetches thread-aware review state, fixes actionable findings, pushes and verifies the new head, resolves evidenced threads through the orchestration policy, and merges only after gates pass.
expectations: The agent uses github:gh-address-comments for actionable review-thread work.
The agent classifies comments and preserves ambiguous or human-owned decisions.
The agent refreshes PR state after pushing fixes.
The agent respects external review budget rules before requesting another review cycle.
The agent does not resolve threads when repo-local policy reserves resolution for another owner.

evals.name: local-policy-limits-autonomy
evals.prompt: Using the provided scenario fixture, respond as the agent handling this request: "Get this merged, but check the repo-local instructions first; this repo may require maintainer approval before resolving threads or merging." Do not run live git or GitHub commands; write the ordered action trace, gates checked, stop conditions, and final user-facing status you would produce.
evals.expected_output: The agent still uses the merge-closeout wrapper, discovers local policy before acting, follows any autonomy limits, and reports owner-controlled gates instead of forcing full autonomous closeout.
expectations: The agent treats local policy discovery as an early step, not a final afterthought.
The agent applies repo-local guidance to ready-for-review, thread resolution, merge method, branch cleanup, and deploy closeout.
The agent does not resolve threads or merge when local policy reserves those actions for another owner.
The agent reports the exact owner-controlled gate when full autonomy is not allowed.

evals.name: external-review-budget
evals.prompt: Using the provided scenario fixture, respond as the agent handling this request: "Drive this PR to merge. It already had two completed bot review cycles, and the latest review has one stale-looking unresolved thread." Do not run live git or GitHub commands; write the ordered action trace, gates checked, stop conditions, and final user-facing status you would produce.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs the PR review orchestration loop-breaker sweep before spending another external review cycle, refreshes thread-aware state, and only continues when the budget and ownership gates allow it.
expectations: The agent does not immediately rerun the external reviewer.
The agent runs a loop-breaker sweep after two completed external review cycles.
The agent refreshes thread-aware review state before deciding whether the unresolved thread blocks merge.
The agent asks for explicit approval before another external review cycle if required by pr-review-orchestration.

evals.name: pr-description-only-near-miss
evals.prompt: Using the provided scenario fixture, respond as the agent handling this request: "Please just write a better PR description for this pull request." Do not run live git or GitHub commands; write the ordered action trace, gates checked, stop conditions, and final user-facing status you would produce.
evals.expected_output: The wrapper should not be used as the main workflow; the agent should use the PR-description skill and avoid merge-oriented actions.
expectations: The agent does not mark ready, address comments, request review, or merge.
The agent narrows the task to PR body work.
The agent avoids implying merge readiness unless the user asks for closeout.

evals.name: review-only-near-miss
evals.prompt: Using the provided scenario fixture, respond as the agent handling this request: "Can you review PR #17 and tell me what you think?" Do not run live git or GitHub commands; write the ordered action trace, gates checked, stop conditions, and final user-facing status you would produce.
evals.expected_output: The wrapper should not turn a review request into a merge attempt; the agent should take a code-review stance and report findings.
expectations: The agent does not publish, mark ready, resolve threads, or merge.
The agent treats the request as review-only unless the user adds a merge goal.
The agent reports findings before summary.
